# Morning Brief — April 10, 2026

**Generated by:** /morning-brief
**Brand:** TaskFlow
**Campaign:** Q2 Awareness Push (Week 3)

---

## Today's Recommended Content

### Platform: Instagram
**Format:** Carousel (5 slides)

---

### Headline
**3 Ways TaskFlow Saves Your Team 2 Hours Every Week**

### Subheadline
Small changes. Big time savings. Here's what our users actually did.

### Body

**Slide 1 (Cover):**
"Your team is losing 2 hours a week to project management overhead. Here's how to get them back."

**Slide 2 — Ditch the Status Meeting:**
Replace your Monday status sync with a TaskFlow board check-in. Average time saved: 45 minutes/week. Just pin your board link in Slack and let updates speak for themselves.

**Slide 3 — One-Click Task Handoffs:**
Stop @-mentioning people in chat to hand off work. In TaskFlow, drag the task to the next person. They get notified. Done. Average time saved: 30 minutes/week.

**Slide 4 — Auto-Generated Weekly Recap:**
TaskFlow sends your team a Friday recap email — what shipped, what moved, what's stuck. No one has to write it. Average time saved: 45 minutes/week.

**Slide 5 (CTA):**
"Start your free trial. Your Mondays will thank you. Link in bio."

### Call to Action
**Primary CTA:** "Start free — link in bio"
**Secondary CTA:** "Save this post for your next team standup"

## Brand Voice Check

- Tone: Conversational, direct, helpful-first. Matches voice profile.
- No jargon. No superlatives. Real numbers.
- "Your Mondays will thank you" — light personality without forcing humor.

---

## Notes for the Creator

- Use the Deep Indigo (`#3D52D5`) background with white text for slides 1 and 5. Soft White (`#FAFBFC`) background with Charcoal text for slides 2-4.
- Keep each slide to one idea. No more than 40 words per slide.
- If adapting for Twitter/X: pull slide 2 as a standalone tip tweet with a link to the full carousel on Instagram.
